# Grid General (Insular Imperium)

> A turn-based strategy game set in a Roman/medieval island-warfare setting, built with Phaser 3 and JavaScript (ESM). Players command armies on procedurally generated islands, fighting Orc factions and a Red AI opponent in grid-based tactical combat with naval warfare.

## Tech Stack

- **Game framework:** Phaser 3 (v3.90.0) — rendering, sprites, scenes, input, animations, game loop
- **Audio:** Tone.js — procedural sound effects (hit, heal, level-up, frenzy, etc.)
- **Language:** JavaScript ES Modules (`"type": "module"` in package.json)
- **Desktop packaging:** Electron + electron-builder (Windows/macOS/Linux)
- **Dev server:** Vite (HMR, on-demand serving)
- **Compression:** lz-string (save data)
- **Tile art:** 16×16 MiniWorld sprites with blob-based autotiling

## Project Structure

```
main.js                  — Phaser game bootstrap (creates Phaser.Game with scene list)
index.html               — Entry point, importmap for ESM CDN deps, game container
config.js                — Central config: tile size, map dimensions, economy, build costs,
                           UI layout, modal panel keys, teams, active modes, AI params,
                           terrain, ship classes, unit stats, map generation, PRODUCTION
                           (FORT_SIZE_UNIT_KEYS, PORT_SHIP_KEYS for fort/port build restrictions)
GameState.js             — createGameState() factory: plain object holding all mutable state
                           (units, selection, reachable tiles, combat targets, turn, gold, UI flags)
AudioManager.js          — Tone.js procedural audio (hit, heal, level-up, siege sounds)
MapManager.js            — Map orchestrator: delegates to terrain generator, location placer,
                           pathing service; holds grid, locations, bitmask computation
MapTerrainGenerator.js   — Procedural island generation (water edges, inlets, terrain types)
MapLocationPlacer.js     — Places forts, orc camps, ports, workshops with name pools
MapPathingService.js     — A* pathing (findPath optional options.units/movingUnit to avoid occupied tiles),
                           getReachableTiles (excludes occupied), road generation via MST, bridge placement

scenes/
  MainMenuScene.js       — Title screen, New Game / Load Game / Island Select buttons
  IslandSelectScene.js   — Placeholder for island/faction/difficulty selection
  GameScene.js           — Core gameplay scene (~617 lines): initializes 13+ managers,
                           creates map display, manages turn flow

entities/
  Unit.js                — Base entity class (Phaser Container): HP, ATK, DEF, MOV, level/XP,
                           multi-turn pathing (persistentPath), moveTo, moveAlongPath (step-by-step;
                           resolves with { blocked, stepsCompleted } if next tile occupied), embark
  Hero.js                — Extends Unit: named hero with bonus stats, passive aura (4-tile range),
                           active ability with cooldown, golden badge, portrait, aura ring visuals
  Orc.js                 — Extends Unit: Frenzy mechanic (+50% ATK at ≤50% HP)
  OrcHealer.js           — Orc healer subtype
  OrcMage.js             — Orc mage subtype
  OrcRider.js            — Orc mounted subtype
  OrcSpear.js            — Orc spear subtype

managers/
  AIManager.js           — Enemy turn AI for Red and Orc; camera startFollow(unit) during each unit's move
  AnimationManager.js    — Unit animations, visual effects; mage spell (lightning) — undead mage uses bright green
  CameraManager.js       — Map scrolling, zoom, tile cursors (including road-placement cursor)
  CombatManager.js       — Attack resolution, flanking, damage calculation, stat breakdowns
  EconomyManager.js      — Gold income, maintenance, loot, purchase transactions
  EngineerManager.js     — Workshop/road/bridge/castra construction, engineer staffing
  HeroManager.js         — Hero selection, aura application, ability activation (9-hero roster)
  InputManager.js        — Click/keyboard handling, selection; path for immediate move uses unit-avoidance;
                           distant path (Path Move) ignores units; blocked result → "Blocked" message
  NavalManager.js        — Ship lifecycle facade, delegates to 7 naval sub-modules
  ProductionManager.js   — Production queues; getBuildableUnitKeys/getBuildableShipKeys by fort size/port
                           (CONFIG.PRODUCTION); red fort spawn and blue completion validate buildable roster
  SaveManager.js         — Serialization/deserialization with swappable storage backends
  SiegeManager.js        — Siege unit logic (catapults/ballistae) — stub/WIP
  SpawnManager.js        — Initial army deployment and unit spawning
  TurnManager.js         — Turn cycling (Blue → Red → Orc), Path Move / Move All (camera follows
                           moving unit), processAutoMovePaths (blocked handling, persistent path trim)
  UIManager.js           — HUD facade: delegates to managers/ui/ (MedievalUI, MapHighlightUI,
                           ManageCrewPanel, StatPanelUI, ProductionReportUI, ShipPlacementConfirmUI)

managers/naval/
  NavalAI.js             — AI ship turn logic (aggro, movement, bombard targeting)
  ShipCombat.js          — Ship bombard targeting, execution, damage to ships/units/locations
  ShipCrew.js            — Embark, disembark, deck/below-deck, crew transfer, reorder, promote
  ShipMovement.js        — Ship movement, turning, reachable tile computation
  ShipPlacement.js       — Ship creation, placement validation, placement preview
  ShipState.js           — Sinking, derelict, repair, naval state advancement
  ShipVisuals.js         — Ship sprite creation and visual updates

managers/storage/
  StorageAdapter.js      — Abstract storage interface
  WebStorageAdapter.js   — localStorage-based save/load
  ElectronStorageAdapter.js — File-based save/load via Electron IPC
  getStorageAdapter.js   — Factory: returns Electron or Web adapter based on environment

managers/ui/
  ConfirmDialogUI.js     — Reusable confirmation dialog with Yes/No
  ComingSoonPanel.js     — "Coming Soon" placeholder modal
  HeroCardPopup.js       — Hero detail card with stats, abilities, aura info
  ManageCrewPanel.js     — Ship crew management panel
  MapHighlightUI.js      — Map tile highlighting (movement, attack range)
  MapSizePanel.js        — Map size selection for new game
  MedievalUI.js          — Shared medieval-themed UI button/panel factory
  ModalHelper.js         — Reusable modal panel creation (blocker + container + destroy cleanup)
  PauseOptionsPanel.js   — Pause menu with save/load/options
  ProductionReportUI.js  — Production overview report
  ShipPlacementConfirmUI.js — Ship placement confirmation dialog
  StatBreakdown.js       — Combat stat breakdown data
  StatBreakdownPanel.js  — Visual stat breakdown panel
  StatPanelUI.js         — Unit stat display panel

electron/
  main.cjs               — Electron main process
  preload.cjs            — Electron preload script (IPC bridge for file storage)

assets/
  test-map.json          — Test map data
  tiles/                 — Tile sprite assets and blob lookup tables
    blob_lookup.js       — Bitmask → sprite frame mapping
    blob_lookup.json     — Same as JSON

documentation/           — Design docs, feature plans, implementation notes
tools/                   — Python scripts for generating tile sprite sheets
```

## Architecture & Key Patterns

### Scene Flow
MainMenuScene → (optional) IslandSelectScene → GameScene

### State Management
`GameState` is a plain object factory (`createGameState()`) holding all mutable game state. Managers receive it on construction. Scene keeps backward-compatible aliases (`scene.selectedUnit`, etc.) during ongoing migration.

### Manager Pattern
GameScene creates and owns 13+ manager instances. Each manager receives `scene` (for Phaser APIs) and operates on shared state. Naval functionality is decomposed into 7 sub-modules under `managers/naval/` with `NavalManager` as a facade.

### Modal System
`ModalHelper.createModalPanel()` handles blocker creation, depth management, and cleanup. `CONFIG.MODAL_PANEL_KEYS` is the single source of truth for which panels exist (used by InputManager for Escape and CameraManager for scroll-blocking).

### Config-Driven Design
Nearly all tunable values live in `config.js`: unit stats, terrain costs/effects, map generation parameters, economy values, AI behavior, UI layout constants, ship classes. Terrain types define movement cost, defense bonuses, damage modifiers, and healing.

### Map Generation
Procedural island generation with retry logic for naval connectivity:
1. `MapTerrainGenerator` — creates island shape with water edges, inlets, terrain distribution
2. `MapLocationPlacer` — places named forts, orc camps, ports, workshops with distance constraints
3. `MapPathingService` — generates road network via MST connecting forts, A* pathing

### Combat System
Grid-based with flanking bonuses, terrain modifiers (forest damage reduction, fort defense bonus, bridge damage bonus), siege units, and naval bombardment. Heroes provide passive aura buffs to nearby allies and have active abilities with cooldowns. Mage spell animation: red/blue team colors; undead mage uses same lightning in bright green.

### Economy
Territory-based income (forts/camps generate gold per turn), unit maintenance costs, loot from capturing tiles, construction costs. Initial army-assembly budget of 2,500g. AI follows the same economic rules.

### Movement & Pathing
- Units move step-by-step along paths (`Unit.moveAlongPath`). Reachable tiles exclude occupied tiles (no moving through or ending on another unit).
- **Path planning** for distant targets (Path Move / persistent path) ignores other units; when executing, if a step is blocked by another unit, movement stops, "Blocked" is shown, and the path is trimmed. Immediate move (click reachable tile) uses pathfinding with unit avoidance.
- **Camera:** Red/Orc turn — camera follows the active unit. Blue Path Move and Move All — camera follows the moving unit step-by-step.

### Production Restrictions
Forts: buildable land units depend on fort size (`CONFIG.PRODUCTION.FORT_SIZE_UNIT_KEYS`; 9/8-tile get full roster; smaller forts fewer types). Ports build only ships (Liburna, Galley, Trireme). Red fort auto-spawn and blue production validate against these.

### Naval System
Ships are multi-tile floating terrain (Liburna 1-tile, Galley 2-tile, Trireme 3-tile) with deck/below-deck crew slots, hull HP, bombardment range, and boarding/disembarking mechanics.

### Save System
Pluggable storage: `WebStorageAdapter` (localStorage) for browser, `ElectronStorageAdapter` (file-based via IPC) for desktop. Save data compressed with lz-string.

## Development

```bash
npm install          # Install dependencies
npm run dev          # Start Vite dev server (browser)
npm start            # Run in Electron desktop window
npm run build        # Package with electron-builder
```

## Conventions

- All game code is JavaScript ESM (import/export)
- No TypeScript, no build step for game code (Vite serves directly)
- Config values go in `config.js`, not scattered in managers
- New UI panels should use `ModalHelper.createModalPanel()` and register in `CONFIG.MODAL_PANEL_KEYS`
- Terrain bitmask computation lives in `MapManager`
- Entity classes extend `Unit` (Phaser Container subclass)
- Teams are `'blue'` (player), `'red'` (AI enemy), `'orc'` (AI enemy) — defined in `CONFIG.TEAMS`
- Active modes (attack, heal, build, etc.) defined in `CONFIG.ACTIVE_MODES`

## Known Architecture Notes

- ~18k lines of JavaScript across 57+ files
- Largest files: ProductionManager, UIManager, CombatManager, InputManager, SpawnManager; GameScene, ManageCrewPanel, PauseOptionsPanel, config, AnimationManager, Unit, MapPathingService (see documentation/js-files-line-count.md)
- UIManager refactored to delegate to managers/ui/ (MedievalUI, MapHighlightUI, ManageCrewPanel, StatPanelUI, ProductionReportUI, ShipPlacementConfirmUI)
- Managers still read/write `scene.*` properties heavily; migration to GameState is partial
- SiegeManager and EconomyManager are small stubs / WIP
- See `plan.md` and `refactor-2-25-26.md` for refactoring roadmap
- For full config schema and embedded documentation (difficulty, engineer, etc.), see `llms-full.txt`
